scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every practical GET list/detail endpoint (verified against the official webscraper.io API documentation and the webscraperio/api-client-php reference client, which mirrors the real wire paths/response shapes exactly) is now covered_by a stream, and every real create/update/delete mutation is covered_by a writes.json action. Legacy's original 2 streams (`sitemaps`/`jobs`, singular /sitemap and /scraping-job paths with no id) are kept byte-for-byte as-is for parity even though the real documented listing endpoints are the plural /sitemaps and /scraping-jobs paths — see docs.md Known limits for the discrepancy this surfaced.

excluded.reason: single-sitemap detail lookup returns the same {id, name, sitemap} shape already reachable via the sitemaps/sitemaps_list list streams' individual records; no incremental value as a separate stream without a fan_out id-list config surface duplicating sitemaps_list's own output
single-scraping-job detail lookup returns the same object shape already reachable via the jobs/scraping_jobs_list list streams' individual records

returns a newline-delimited-JSON bulk data export file (one scraped record per line, arbitrary caller-defined selector schema), not a single structured object this dialect's records.path/schema projection can describe
returns a raw CSV export file (arbitrary caller-defined selector-driven columns), not JSON
returns a raw XLSX binary export file, not JSON

undocumented on the public API reference (only exposed via the community PHP SDK's getScrapingJobDataQuality helper with no published response schema); no authoritative field-level source to build a schema from
undocumented on the public API reference (only exposed via the community PHP SDK's scheduler helpers with no published response schema); no authoritative field-level source to build a schema from
undocumented on the public API reference (community PHP SDK only, no published request/response schema)
undocumented on the public API reference (community PHP SDK only, no published request/response schema)
